summary: Round 4 quick smoke test. /login redirect + csrf cookie are correct. However TWO blockers prevent any authenticated UI flow from being exercised: (1) Postgres is NOT running at localhost:5432, so credential login fails with Configuration error; (2) /login-form client component renders forever as 'Preparing…' — hidden csrfToken input stays empty even though /api/auth/csrf returns 200 from the browser. As a result none of the dashboard / doc / snapshot / AI / presence / RBAC scenarios could be reached.
passed: GET /login 302-redirects to /login-form and sets __Host-authjs.csrf-token cookie (Secure, Path=/) — visible in browser cookie jar
Hocuspocus server reachable on localhost:1234 (HTTP 200, node /app/hocuspocus/server.cjs is running, spawned by backend)
Footer renders with footer-author-name='Pritam Roy Choudhury', GitHub + LinkedIn links visible on /login-form

failed.issue: Postgres is not running at localhost:5432. Prisma throws 'Can't reach database server at localhost:5432' inside NextAuth authorize(). Result: POST /api/auth/callback/credentials with valid csrfToken + correct creds (test@inkwell.dev / test1234) → 302 to /api/auth/error?error=Configuration. NO test user can log in.
failed.evidence: supervisorctl shows no postgres process (only mongodb, backend, frontend, hocuspocus). frontend.err.log: 'prisma:error … Can't reach database server at localhost:5432'. curl POST callback returns redirect to /api/auth/error?error=Configuration.

failed.issue: Login submit button stays disabled with text 'Preparing…' indefinitely. Hidden <input name=csrfToken> value remains empty string. The useEffect fetch to /api/auth/csrf does not appear to populate React state (no setErr triggered either). From an injected fetch in the same page context /api/auth/csrf returns 200 with a valid token, so the network is fine — looks like a client hydration / dev-mode (Turbopack) issue with the useEffect path. This blocks the entire UI sign-in flow even if Postgres were up.
failed.evidence: page.evaluate("document.querySelector('input[name=csrfToken]').value") → '' (NONE). Button data-testid='login-submit' is disabled, innerText='Preparing…' after 20s. Browser cookies show __Host-authjs.csrf-token is present. Console only shows webpack-hmr WS 502 noise, no app errors, and login-error testid is not rendered.

action_items: Start/restore Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgresql://docedit:docedit@localhost:5432/docedit). The previous rounds clearly had it running; it appears to have died or never been re-launched after a backend restart. Add it to supervisord (or whatever supervises pg) so it auto-recovers.

Investigate why /login-form's useEffect → setCsrf path does not run in the served build. Two likely root causes: (a) JS bundle for /login-form failing to hydrate (check that the page actually mounts — add a console.log in useEffect to confirm); (b) React StrictMode double-invoke + Turbopack dev cache returning a stale/failed promise. A quick fix is to either fetch the csrfToken on the server (Server Component) and pass it as a prop, OR render the form fully unguarded and let Auth.js itself validate the token; do NOT gate the submit button on local csrf state.
After both fixes land, full presence / RBAC / AI test (the bulk of this iteration) needs to be re-run from scratch.

success_rate.backend: 60% (3/5 reachable checks pass; auth + tenant-isolation untested due to no working login)
success_rate.frontend: 10% (only /login redirect + footer verified; every authenticated flow blocked)
